japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
reading: I no naka no kawazu taikai o shirazu
english: A frog in a well knows nothing of the great ocean.
A three-day monk (someone who quits easily).

reading: Ishi no ue ni mo san-nen
english: Three years on a stone (perseverance brings success).
If you are in a hurry, take the long way around.
Dumplings rather than flowers (substance over style).
Gold coins to a cat (something valuable wasted on someone who doesn't appreciate it).
Fall seven times, stand up eight.

japanese: 二兎を追う者は一兎をも得ず
reading: Nito o ou mono wa itto o mo ezu
english: He who chases two rabbits catches neither.
Seeing once is better than hearing a hundred times.

reading: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to the smiling house.
